text: Some time later Joseph was told his father was ill, so he took his two sons Manasseh and Ephraim with him.
When Jacob was told his son Joseph had come, he rallied his strength and sat up on the bed.
Jacob said to Joseph, God Almighty appeared to me at Luz in Canaan and blessed me.
He said he would make me fruitful, increase my numbers, and give this land to my descendants as an everlasting possession.
Your two sons born in Egypt before I came will be reckoned as mine, just as Reuben and Simeon are mine.
Any children born to you after them will be yours, and will be listed under their brothers' names for inheritance.
As I was returning from Paddan, Rachel died on the way, some distance from Ephrath, and I buried her there on the way to Ephrath, that is, Bethlehem.
When Israel saw Joseph's sons, he asked, who are these?
Joseph told his father they were the sons God had given him there; Israel said, bring them to me so I may bless them.
Israel's eyes were failing because of age, and he could hardly see; Joseph brought his sons close, and he kissed and embraced them.
Israel said to Joseph, I never expected to see your face again, and now God has let me see your children too.
Joseph took them from Israel's knees and bowed down with his face to the ground.
He positioned Ephraim on his right toward Israel's left hand, and Manasseh on his left toward Israel's right hand, and brought them close.
But Israel crossed his arms and put his right hand on Ephraim's head, though he was younger, and his left hand on Manasseh's, though he was firstborn.
He blessed Joseph, saying, may the God before whom my fathers walked, the God who has been my shepherd all my life, bless these boys.
May the Angel who has delivered me from all harm bless them; may they be called by my name and my fathers' names, and may they increase greatly on the earth.
When Joseph saw his father's hand on Ephraim's head, he was displeased and tried to move it to Manasseh's head.
Joseph said, no, my father, this one is the firstborn; put your right hand on his head.
His father refused, saying, I know, my son, I know; he too will become a people and be great, but his younger brother will be greater, and his descendants will become a group of nations.
He blessed them that day, saying, in your name Israel will pronounce this blessing: may God make you like Ephraim and Manasseh; he put Ephraim ahead of Manasseh.
Israel said to Joseph, I am about to die, but God will be with you and take you back to the land of your fathers.
I give you, as one who is over your brothers, the mountain slope I took from the Amorites with my sword and my bow.
